title: Internal error with timing control in while-break loop
body:
Hi! I encountered a internal error while playing around with the new timing feature.

> Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

here is a minimal example `t_timing_break.sv` (it works fine with vivado's xsim):
``` systemverilog
module t();
  logic clk = 0;
  logic out = 1;

  always #5 clk = ~clk;

  initial begin
    while(1) begin
      if(out) begin
        break;
      end
      @(negedge clk);
    end

    $finish();
  end
endmodule
```

result of command `verilator --debug --timing --binary t_timing_break.sv`:

``` log
Starting Verilator 5.003 devel rev v5.002-13-ga29d9469d
- Verilator.cpp:586:  Option --verilate: Start Verilation

.......

- V3Ast.cpp:1121:     Dumping obj_dir/Vt_timming_break_030_const.tree
%Error: Internal Error: t_timming_break.sv:10:9: ../V3Broken.cpp:172: Broken link in node (or something without maybePointedTo): '!labelp()->brokeExistsBelow()' @ ../V3AstNodes.cpp:1604
-node: JUMPGO 0x555556116980 <e204> {c10aj} -> JUMPLABEL 0x5555561168c0 <e247> {c8af} -> JUMPBLOCK 0x5555561167f0 <e249#> {c8af}
   10 |         break;
      |         ^~~~~
                        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
- V3Ast.cpp:1121:     Dumping obj_dir/Vt_timming_break_990_final.tree
- V3Broken.cpp:326:   Broken called under broken, skipping recursion.
- V3StatsReport.cpp:223:statsReport: 
%Error: Internal Error: Aborting since under --debug
%Error: export VERILATOR_ROOT=/home/jomit/usr
%Error: setarch --addr-no-randomize /home/jomit/usr/bin/verilator_bin_dbg --debug --timing --binary t_timming_break.sv
%Error: Command Failed setarch --addr-no-randomize /home/jomit/usr/bin/verilator_bin_dbg --debug --timing --binary t_timming_break.sv
```

> What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

using latest git master version `Verilator 5.003 devel rev v5.002-13-ga29d9469d`

> What OS and distribution are you using?

Manjaro 22.0.0

> May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

no, I'm not familiar with verilator source code.
